data.title: ImmunityBio Lead Bladder Cancer Drug Trial Clears Power Check
data.description:
ImmunityBio Inc. (NASDAQ:IBRX) shares are down during Thursday’s premarket session. Year to date, the stock has jumped over 300%.

The company announced that the Independent Data Monitoring Committee (IDMC) confirmed the statistical power of its pivotal randomized trial for Anktiva.

Pivotal Trial Deemed Adequately Powered

The IDMC’s review indicated that no additional enrollment is needed beyond the current 366 participants, confirming that the study is adequately powered to detect a clinically meaningful difference in complete response (CR) rates.

The trial, known as QUILT-2.005, is evaluating Anktiva in combination with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) for patients with non-muscle invasive bladder cancer with carcinoma in situ (CIS) with or without papillary disease.

Interim Analysis Supports Study Design

Upon reaching this pre-specified 50% evaluable threshold, a planned interim analysis by an IDMC was initiated per protocol to verify that the 366 patients enrolled to date provide sufficient statistical power to detect the pre-specified clinically meaningful difference in CR rate between the two arms.

In the context of this trial, ImmunityBio has highlighted the promising results from earlier phases, where patients showed a prolonged duration of complete remission.

Regulatory Path And Pipeline Progress

The company plans to submit a supplemental Biologics License Application based on the final data analysis in 2026.

Additionally, the company is advancing its phase 1 trial (QUILT-3.076). It will combine M-ceNK cells with Anktiva, which has demonstrated promising antitumor activity across multiple cancer types.

On Tuesday, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) determined that a direct-to-consumer TV ad and podcast promoting Anktiva were false or misleading and misbranded the drug under federal law. 

The FDA said the materials made unsupported claims that Anktiva could cure or prevent cancer and suggested effectiveness beyond its approved use, including treatment for multiple cancers and use as a standalone therapy. 

Last week, the National Comprehensive Cancer Network updated its Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology for Bladder Cancer and included Anktiva plus BCG for BCG-unresponsive NMIBC with papillary-only disease.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $12.57. Recent analyst moves include:

  • D. Boral Capital: Buy (Maintains Target to $23.00) (Mar. 23)
  • BTIG: Buy (Target $13.00) (Mar. 12)

Top ETF Exposure

  • Virtus LifeSci Biotech Products ETF (NYSE:BBP): 4.03% Weight

IBRX Stock Price Activity: ImmunityBio shares were down 2.44% at $7.87 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: 'We'll Create Your Own Marketing Team': Mark Cuban Says AI Is Replacing SaaS
data.description:
A new wave of AI-powered "agents" is beginning to replace traditional Software-as-a-Service software, reshaping how businesses buy and use technology, according to billionaire entrepreneur Mark Cuban. 

AI agents can "create your own marketing team… and do all these different things for you that you no longer have to do," Cuban said last week on the technology podcast "TBPN", describing a new class of services that operate more like outsourced teams than traditional software.

Don't Miss:

The shift, he said, comes from a simple change. Companies that learn to build and deploy AI agents can replicate entire business functions that once required multiple tools and employees.

"Once you figure out how to do agents… you can turn that into what would have been a SaaS business in the past," Cuban said

From Software To Execution

For Cuban, the appeal is straightforward, driven by lower costs and faster deployment. With AI handling execution, the marginal cost of launching these services has dropped significantly.

"The marginal cost to start is so low and it's so fast," Cuban said on the podcast.

Trending: Skip the Regrets: The Essential Retirement Tips Experts Wish Everyone Knew Earlier.

Early Traction, Not Proven Growth

Despite the surge in new AI-driven startups, Cuban cautioned that most are still in the early stages. When asked whether these businesses are growing revenue or profit, he told "TBPN" they are "just trying to get some traction."

Despite the surge in new AI-driven startups, Cuban said most are still in the early stages. When asked whether these businesses are growing revenue or profit, he said they are "just trying to get some traction."

"In a world where software can be created in seconds, the old model breaks," he added.

Workforce Impact And Cost Pressure

Cuban also pointed to a more disruptive implication, saying startups are "trying to turn that into replace all your employees so you can start up or you can cut costs." 

The shift also signals a change in how companies are structured, with entire functions increasingly packaged into AI agents as employees rather than built as internal teams. If the trend continues, businesses may no longer need to buy software to get work done.

As artificial intelligence reshapes how work gets done—potentially replacing entire functions once handled by teams—some companies are also rethinking the environments where that work happens. Platforms like Immersed are building virtual workspaces designed to boost productivity and collaboration, reflecting a broader shift in how technology is redefining the modern workplace.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Honeywell Teams with Rhombus to Modernize Building Security
data.description:
This strategic partnership aims to address the rising demand for integrated cloud access and video solutions, adding pressure as broader markets edged lower.

Partners Rhombus On Cloud Security

Honeywell and Rhombus will deliver an integrated access control and video management solution that is easy to deploy and manage.

This collaboration is set to support a variety of commercial environments, including retail chains and schools, with Rhombus products initially available through Honeywell in North America.

The partnership is part of Honeywell’s ongoing effort to modernize building security, which began with the acquisition of LenelS2 in 2024.

Notably, the cloud video solutions market is projected to grow at over 20% annually (as per Omdia), indicating a strong demand for such innovations in the security industry.

Commits $500M In Defense Deal

On Wednesday, Honeywell disclosed a deal of $500 million to upgrade its production capacity under this new agreement. This marks a significant investment in defense technologies.

This framework agreement positions Honeywell Aerospace as a key supplier, enabling the company to meet urgent demands for critical capabilities in national defense.

The agreement highlights Honeywell's focus on delivering advanced technologies across various sectors, including aerospace, defense, and industrial automation.

HON 3.8% Below 20-Day SMA

The broader market experienced declines on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 falling 0.84%. Honeywell’s stock decline occurred as the industrials sector also faced pressure, suggesting that the company’s movement may be influenced by broader market trends.

The stock is currently trading 3.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 2.8% below its 50-day SMA, indicating short-term weakness. However, Honeywell shares have increased 12.41% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 41.31, which is considered neutral territory. Meanwhile, MACD is at -2.8838, below its signal line at -0.7471, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $245.50
  • Key Support: $193.00

HON Q1 Update Set for April 23

Honeywell International, Inc. is slated to provide its next financial update on April 23, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: $2.32 (Down from $2.51)
  • Revenue Estimate: $9.27 Billion (Down from $9.82 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 32.5x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $252.17. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Morgan Stanley: Equal-Weight (Raises Target to $245.00) (Mar. 12)
  • Jefferies: Hold (Raises Target to $245.00) (Mar. 4)
  • Barclays: Overweight (Raises Target to $275.00) (Mar. 4)

HON ETF Weights: NATO 5.38%, PPA 4.40%

Significance: Because HON carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

HON Stock Price Activity: Honeywell Intl shares were down 0.66% at $224.30 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Dow Falls 250 Points; US Initial Jobless Claims Rise
data.description:
U.S. stocks traded lower this morning, with the Dow Jones index falling more than 250 points on Thursday.

Following the market opening Thursday, the Dow traded down 0.56% to 46,171.31 while the NASDAQ fell 1.15% to 21,677.52. The S&P 500 also fell, dropping, 0.82% to 6,537.54.

Leading and Lagging Sectors

Energy shares climbed by 0.6% on Thursday.

In trading on Thursday, communication services stocks fell by 1.6%.

Top Headline

U.S. initial jobless claims increased by 5,000 from the previous month to 210,000 during the third week of March, in-line with the median market estimates.

Equities Trading UP
           

Equities Trading DOWN

Commodities

In commodity news, oil traded up 3.9% to $93.88 while gold traded down 2.6% at $4,436.00.

Silver traded down 5.8% to $68.415 on Thursday, while copper fell 0.9% to $5.5085.

Euro zone

European shares were lower today. The eurozone's STOXX 600 fell 1.2%, while Spain's IBEX 35 Index fell 1.3%. London's FTSE 100 declined 1.3%, Germany's DAX dipped 1.6% and France's CAC 40 fell 1% during the session.

Asia Pacific Markets

Asian markets closed lower on Thursday, with Japan's Nikkei 225 falling 0.27%, Hong Kong's Hang Seng index declining 1.89% and China's Shanghai Composite dropping 1.09%.

Economics

U.S. initial jobless claims increased by 5,000 from the previous month to 210,000 during the third week of March, in-line with the median market estimates.

Photo via Shutterstock
